text: Patient presents for visit prompted by aunt's list: prediabetes follow-up, gum concerns, and vaccinations
transcript_quote: The sugar thing, from the letter last year. My gums, because she noticed. And she wrote get your shots in capital letters.

text: Hemoglobin A1c is 6.24%, in the prediabetes range (below 6.5%, above normal)
transcript_quote: It came back six point two four. That is still in the prediabetes range

text: Prediabetes noted last year; patient reports no lifestyle changes were made and was not given guidance on what to do
transcript_quote: It scared me for like two weeks, and then life happened... nobody really told me what to actually do.

text: Family history of diabetes (grandmother)
transcript_quote: My grandma has diabetes, the real kind, with the finger sticks

text: Random blood glucose is 99 mg/dL (normal)
transcript_quote: Your sugar today was ninety-nine, which is fine.

text: Body weight 94.4 kg, height 173 cm, BMI 31.4 kg/m2, in obesity range
transcript_quote: your weight today is ninety-four point four kilos, height one seventy-three, so your body mass index is thirty-one point four. That crosses into the obesity range

text: Obesity (BMI 30+) - active condition
Counseled to swap sugary drinks (soda/juice) for fizzy water as primary dietary change for prediabetes
transcript_quote: Swap the sugary drinks first — that is the single biggest one... Juice is soda with better marketing

text: Counseled to walk after work shift ~20 minutes and build meals around beans, rice, and frozen vegetables
transcript_quote: Walking after your shift, even twenty minutes. And building meals around the cheap boring staples, beans and rice and frozen vegetables

text: Referral to free nutrition class (covered by insurance)
transcript_quote: I can also refer you to the nutrition class here — it is free with your coverage.

text: Known allergies: mold, dust mites, animal dander, grass and tree pollen, plus aspirin reaction

slots.allergens: mold, dust mites, animal dander, grass and tree pollen, aspirin

transcript_quote: The chart says mold, dust mites, animal dander, grass and tree pollen — basically the outdoors and half the indoors — plus the aspirin reaction.

text: Continue cetirizine 5 mg orally once every morning through allergy seasons
transcript_quote: You take the cetirizine, five milligrams?... Keep the cetirizine daily through your bad seasons

text: Patient reports rough spring allergy season with eye symptoms through April, controlled with cetirizine
transcript_quote: Spring was rough this year, my eyes were a mess through April, but the pill mostly keeps it livable.

text: Continue carrying EpiPen; not expired, good until next year
transcript_quote: I still have the EpiPen, too. I carry it in my backpack... It is good until next year

text: Counseled to avoid aspirin and NSAIDs (ibuprofen family); check with pharmacist before new pain medications
transcript_quote: no aspirin, and be careful with the related painkillers, the ibuprofen family; check with a pharmacist before anything new for pain.

text: Depression screen (PHQ-2) negative, score 0
transcript_quote: basically nothing on the depression screen

text: Anxiety screen (GAD-7) score 1, minimal; patient reports normal pre-exam anxiety that resolves
transcript_quote: one point on the anxiety one

text: Domestic abuse/safety screen (HARK) negative, score 0; patient denies humiliation, fear, sexual coercion, or physical violence
transcript_quote: No. Nothing like that. House is loud but safe.

text: Alcohol screen (AUDIT-C) score 1; patient reports rare alcohol use (~one drink at occasions)
transcript_quote: Rarely. A cousin's birthday, maybe one drink.

text: Never smoked tobacco; denies vaping, marijuana, or illicit pills
transcript_quote: None. Never smoked anything.

text: Heart rate 90/min, regular (slightly elevated, attributed to walk from bus)
transcript_quote: heart is regular, a touch quick at ninety but you mentioned the walk from the bus

text: Blood pressure 121/62 mmHg
transcript_quote: Blood pressure one twenty-one over sixty-two — good.

text: Lungs clear on exam
transcript_quote: Lungs clear.

text: Exam shows puffy, red gums that bleed on touch, consistent with gingivitis; patient reports gums bleed a lot with brushing
transcript_quote: Your gums are puffy and red along the edges, and they bleed at a touch — that is gingivitis. Do they bleed when you brush? PT: Yeah, kind of a lot lately.

text: Last dental visit was in middle school; dental care never accessible
transcript_quote: Middle school? Dental was never a thing we could fit.

text: Referral to dental clinic downtown for cleaning (accepts Medicaid, no out-of-pocket cost)
transcript_quote: I am sending a referral to the dental clinic downtown — they take your coverage

text: Counseled to floss daily for gingivitis reversal
transcript_quote: That plus flossing and this is gone in a couple of months.

text: CBC normal, no anemia (Hemoglobin 12.66 g/dL, Hematocrit 45.6%)
transcript_quote: your blood count from this morning looks fine — no anemia, nothing alarming.

text: Influenza vaccine administered today
transcript_quote: Flu, a tetanus booster, hepatitis B... and the meningitis vaccine.

text: Td (tetanus) booster administered today
transcript_quote: a tetanus booster

text: Hepatitis B vaccine administered today (relevant for future clinical settings in nursing program)
transcript_quote: hepatitis B — and because your program will eventually put you in clinical settings, that one matters

text: Meningococcal (MCV4P) vaccine administered today
transcript_quote: the meningitis vaccine

text: Social isolation - patient reports living with 6 people but has little social contact; loneliness worse on Sundays, feels less like a choice lately
transcript_quote: lately it feels less like a choice, if that makes sense. Sundays especially. Everyone in the house has their people, and I am just... there.

text: Counseled/referred to community center free study group and walking group; patient chose to try Sunday walking group
transcript_quote: The community center by the transit hub runs a study group and a walking group, both free... The walking group would hit two birds, honestly.

text: Follow-up: recheck Hemoglobin A1c in about 6 months
transcript_quote: we recheck the A1c in about six months

text: SDOH: patient is a college student pursuing associate degree/nursing, works early shifts at grocery store, lives with aunt's family (6 in household), Medicaid coverage, income ~$18,297/year
transcript_quote: I take classes... And I work at the grocery store, the early shift some days. I live at my aunt's place with her family, there are six of us in the house.

text: Patient reports feeling housing/food constraint: store discount is mostly on unhealthy foods
transcript_quote: the store discount is mostly on the wrong foods

text: Medication reconciliation completed
Basic metabolic panel normal (creatinine 1.32 mg/dL, glucose 99, electrolytes within range)
Pain severity reported 1/10
Respiratory rate 15/min
